<!-- Generated by the rsc-client-milestone1-numbers workflow, 2026-06-01. -->

# Milestone 1 — measured results

## 1. Headline

We now **know**, by measurement rather than estimate, that the security-critical net layer is real and correct: every Tier-0 crypto/protocol constant we asserted (**48/48**) is byte-faithful to the vanilla jar `rsclassic-1091943135.jar`, the deob's ISAAC/XTEA/RSA/Buffer primitives produce **byte-identical output** to two independent oracles (mudclient204 and OpenRSC rev-235), and — the strongest single result — the deob primitives match the jar's *own obfuscated classes* (`o`, `tb`, `ja`) **by reflection**, because `client.vh`'s `<clinit>` turns out to complete cleanly on JDK 17 (we expected this path to be blocked). We also know the rscplus toolchain builds green (BUILD SUCCESSFUL, 0 errors) and its ASM patcher cleanly rewrites all **71/71** jar classes, which independently corroborates our identification of `ja.K` as the 1024-bit RSA modulus and locates the exponent in `s.c`. What we still **don't** know: the deob does **not** yet compile as a subsystem (**0/8** packages clean, 1158 errors), but the root cause is structural (a flat-jar→subpackage *visibility* split), not obfuscation drift — genuine residual drift narrows to ~7 signature sites. We have **zero measured confidence** on the rendering and Mudclient/game-logic surfaces: those were not exercised by Milestone 1 and remain estimates. In short: the protocol floor is proven; the build packaging and the upper two-thirds of the client are still open.

## 2. What we ran

| Gate | Result | The number | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| rscplus build (Option B) | **PASS** | 82 sources → 230 `.class`, 0 errors, 66 warnings, 2s | `ant clean dist` → `BUILD SUCCESSFUL`; `dist/rscplus.jar` = 10,284,002 bytes; manifest `Main-Class: Client.Launcher`; JDK 17.0.19 |
| Patch-probe | **PASS** | 71 / 71 classes patched, 0 failures | `JClassPatcher.getInstance().patch()` over every `.class` in the vanilla jar; named targets `ua/e/qa/m/client/f/da/lb/sa/wb/pb` all fired; 6 targets `javap`-verified present |
| Constant audit | **PASS** | 48 / 48 matched, 0 mismatch | `javap` of obf `o/tb/ja/b` + reflective clinit; ISAAC 27, Buffer/XTEA+framing 13, BitBuffer RSA modulus+radix, Packet 6, plus 6 XOR string-pool round-trips |
| Subsystems compile | **FAIL** | 0 / 8 packages clean; 1158 errors (net 335, scene 195, audio 153, shell 121, world 104, util 100, data 88, ui 62, nativeapi **0**) | `javac` JDK 17 whole-subsystem; 329 "not public" + ~753 cascades + 22 Mudclient; after widening types→public, genuine drift = **7 signature sites** |
| ISAAC equivalence | **PASS** | 64 words × 2 seeds | deob == mudclient204 == OpenRSC `orsc.net.Isaac`; zero-seed first word `0x182600f3` = published ISAAC test vector |
| Buffer/BitBuffer round-trips | **PASS** | widths {1,2,3,5,7,8,11,13,16,19,24,31,32} | put↔get identity for byte/short/int3/int/long/signed/smart/varlen; `readBits` vs mudclient204 `Utility.getBitMask` |
| XTEA | **PASS** | byte-identical | `Buffer.teaDecrypt` == OpenRSC `RSBuffer.a(...)`; the previously-flagged `n7 +` term is present (negative-control without it diverges) |
| RSA | **PASS** | 128-byte ct, framed len 130 | `Buffer.rsaEncrypt` == `modPow(exp,mod)` + **2-byte** prefix on jar modulus `ca950472…`; matches OpenRSC rev-235 `encodeWithRSA` (rev-204's 1-byte prefix is wrong rev) |
| Jar-reflection comparison | **PASS** | obf `o`/`tb` × 3 primitives MATCH | URLClassLoader on vanilla jar: obf ISAAC `o`, obf XTEA `tb.a(...)`, obf RSA `tb.a(BigInteger,int,BigInteger)` all == deob (`405143795, 806046349, …`); `client.<clinit>` NOT blocked on JDK 17 |
| Net decompile-diff | **PASS** | empty semantic delta | vineflower diff of deob vs jar `o/tb/ja` after obf normalization (shifts mod 32, `client.vh` branches, profiling counters, dummy params): ISAAC getNextValue/isaac/init, Buffer teaDecrypt, Buffer rsaEncrypt all identical |

## 3. MEASURED confidence (replaces prior estimates)

| Surface | Prior estimate | Measured now | What would close the gap |
|---|---|---|---|
| Protocol / net | ~high (asserted) | **HIGH — proven.** 48/48 constants byte-faithful; 4/4 crypto primitives byte-identical to 2 oracles **and** to the jar's own obf classes by reflection; net decompile-diff = empty semantic delta. Caveat: equivalence proven at the *primitive* level; full opcode/packet-handler parity (the ~100 message IDs in `client`) is not yet diffed. | Per-opcode handler decompile-diff of the `client` message dispatch vs jar; an end-to-end handshake against a live/replay server using the override modulus. |
| Rendering / perception | ~medium (estimate) | **STILL ESTIMATE — 0 measured.** Not exercised in M1. The `ua`/`lb` renderer classes were patch-probed (they rewrite cleanly) but no pixel/output equivalence was run. | Build the render-diff harness (Option C/D): drive deob `Scene`/`GameModel` and jar `ua` on identical model+camera input, compare framebuffers. Note M1 already surfaced 4 of the 7 drift sites here (`scene/GameModel.java:302/304/346`). |
| Mudclient / game-logic | ~low/medium (estimate) | **STILL ESTIMATE — 0 measured, and explicitly out of scope.** The 22 Mudclient compile refs were *excluded*; Mudclient itself was never compiled or run. | Compile + reconcile `Mudclient` (the 22 refs), then state-machine/behavior diff against mudclient204 + jar `client`. Gated behind resolving the subpackage-visibility split first. |

## 4. Bugs / divergences found

**Constant audit: 0 real bugs (0/48 mismatch).** The two transient "null" discrepancies in the XOR string-pool decode were a **documentation-rendering artifact** (a trailing non-printing low byte `0x1e`/`0x7f` made a 4-char encoded literal render as 3 visible chars in a source comment); the jar's raw bytes decode to `"null"` exactly. This proves the constant *values* are correct; it does not prove the surrounding doc comments are all typo-free.

**Equivalence tests: 0 failures.** Notably, two *previously-flagged* concerns were measured to be **non-bugs**:
- The "dropped `n7 +` term" in XTEA — **present and correct**; the negative-control (variant lacking it) diverges, confirming our version is right.
- The "1-byte vs 2-byte RSA length prefix" — the deob's **2-byte** prefix is the correct rev-235 behavior (matches OpenRSC `encodeWithRSA`); the 1-byte form belonged to rev-204.

**Compile errors revealing genuine drift (post-visibility-widening), severity LOW-MEDIUM — 7 signature sites:**
- `net/Packet.java:467`
- `scene/GameModel.java:302`, `:304`, `:346`
- `shell/GameShell.java:658`
- `shell/LoaderThread.java:413` — concrete example: `CachePath.resolveFile(int, null, String, int)` called but declared `(int, String)`
- `world/World.java:877`

These are real arity/signature drifts, not visibility noise. Plus a handful of unimported bare obf-aliases in `net` (`mb`/`i`/`ga`/`w`/`Utility`) — drift inventory, not logic bugs.

**Compile errors that are NOT drift (severity LOW — architectural/cosmetic):** the 329 "not public in client.Y" + ~753 cascades are the flat-jar→subpackage visibility split; the `_` parameter in `ui/Panel.java` is a Java-9 reserved-word issue (fixed). None of these indicate incorrect deobfuscation logic — only packaging.

**What zero bugs does / doesn't prove:** It proves the net layer's constants and crypto/serialization primitives are faithful. It does **not** prove the per-opcode packet handlers, the renderer, or game logic are faithful — those surfaces were not tested.

## 5. Surprises & blockers

- **Biggest surprise — `client.vh` coupling did NOT block jar-reflection.** Prior findings warned that `o`, `tb`, `ja` all open with `getstatic client.vh:Z`, implying you'd need a fully-initialized `client`/applet to touch them. Measured: on JDK 17 the `client`/Mudclient `<clinit>` completes cleanly with no display/graphics, so we could load the obf classes via URLClassLoader and compare them directly to the deob. This upgraded Q2 from "KAT vs external oracles only" to "diff against the jar's own bytecode" — a materially stronger result than planned.
- **Blocker — the deob does not compile as a subsystem (0/8).** Root cause is **architectural, not obf-drift**: the deob split a flat default-package jar into `client.*` subpackages, leaving 47–49 top-level types and many members package-private, so cross-package access fails (329 not-public + ~753 cascades). The packages are mutually circular (`util↔net↔scene↔world↔ui↔data↔audio↔shell`), so there is no clean per-package compile order. `nativeapi` compiles **clean standalone** after dead-dep fixes — proof the toolchain and that package are fine.
- **Dead Microsoft/Netscape deps required surgery:** deleted 4 dead files (`nativeapi/DirectSoundPlayer`, `DirectDrawModes`, `Win32MouseCallback`, `audio/AudioOutput`), stubbed `util/JSBridge` (`netscape.javascript.JSObject`), and rewired `shell/LoaderThread`'s reflective fullscreen/cursor calls to typed calls (fixing drifted method names `enter→enterFullscreen`, `listmodes→listAvailableModes`, etc.).
- **JDK/Ant friction (low):** default `java` on PATH is JDK 26; JDK 17 must be forced via `JAVA_HOME` for every invocation. rscplus emits 66 `[removal]` deprecation warnings (boxed-primitive ctors, `java.applet.*`) — harmless on JDK 17. The worktree branch HEAD predated `reference/`, so the deob sources were copied in from `main` (isolated; main untouched).
- **Measurement caveat:** the "widen all 49 types to public" transform used to isolate drift from visibility noise is a *measurement* transform applied in the worktree, **not** a recommended fix.

## 6. Recommended next gate

**Resolve the subpackage-visibility split so the deob compiles (cheapest, unblocks everything) — then run the per-opcode net handler decompile-diff (highest value on the now-proven surface).**

Rationale from the numbers: the compile blocker is **0/8 but architectural** — the genuine-drift surface is only ~7 sites + a few unimported aliases, meaning the fix is mechanical (collapse the subpackages back toward the flat jar layout, or systematically widen members), not a deobfuscation re-do. This is low-effort and is a hard prerequisite for *any* deeper gate (you cannot render-diff or Mudclient-reconcile code that won't compile). Once it compiles, immediately spend the proven-net momentum on a **per-opcode handler decompile-diff** of `client`'s message dispatch vs the jar — this extends the already-empty net semantic delta from primitives up to the full protocol, and `client.<clinit>` is confirmed loadable so the jar side is available by reflection.

**Mapping to OPTIONS.md:** this is staying the course on **Option B** (deob-as-reference, validated against the jar) and is the natural completion of the net-equivalence work begun here — finish protocol depth before opening new surfaces. **Defer Option C/D (render-diff harness)** until the compile gate is closed; M1 gives it 0 measured confidence and 4 known drift sites (`scene/GameModel.java:302/304/346`) to start from when it's time. **Defer the Mudclient reconciliation** (Option A territory) — it is gated behind the visibility fix and was explicitly out of scope here. Net: one cheap structural fix + one high-value depth diff, both on the surface we just proved, before spending on the two surfaces we have not yet measured.
